zh.lifeSummary: 當James IV Stewart King of Scotland於1473年3月17日出生在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林時，他的父親James III Stewart King of Scotland21歲，母親Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg16歲。他於1503年8月8日與Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安霍利盧德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1502年登記服兵役。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年40歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When James IV Stewart King of Scotland was born on 17 March 1473, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, his father, James III Stewart King of Scotland, was 21 and his mother, Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, was 16. He married Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland on 8 August 1503, in Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1502. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James IV Stewart King of Scotland est né le 17 mars 1473, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, son père, James III Stewart King of Scotland, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland le 8 août 1503, à Holyrood, Midlothian, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1502. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als James IV Stewart King of Scotland am 17. März 1473 in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater James III Stewart King of Scotland 21 und seine Mutter Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg 16. Er hat am 8. August 1503 in Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1502 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England und wurde in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James IV Stewart King of Scotland è nato il il 17 marzo 1473 nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, il suo padre, James III Stewart King of Scotland, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland il 8 agosto 1503, nel Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1502. Morì il 9 settembre 1513, nel Branxton, Northumberland, England, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: James IV Stewart King of Scotlandが1473年3月17日にスコットランドスティルリングシュアイアスティルリングで生まれた時，父James III Stewart King of Scotlandは21歳，母Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburgは16歳であった。1503年8月8日にスコットランドミドロスアンホーリルーッドでMargaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1502年に兵役登録をした。彼は1513年9月9日にイギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンで40歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドにて埋葬された。

ko.Scone Abbey, Perthshire, Scotland: 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 드 스콘 스콘 플리오리
ko.lifeSummary: James IV Stewart King of Scotland(이)가 1473년 3월 17일 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James III Stewart King of Scotland(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1503년 8월 8일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 홀이루드에서 Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1502년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland: Old Scone, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James IV Stewart King of Scotland nasceu em 17 de março de 1473, em Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, seu pai, James III Stewart King of Scotland, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland em 8 de agosto de 1503, em Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1502. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1513, em Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Scone Abbey, Perthshire, Scotland: скоун приори, олд скоун, пиртшир, Шотландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда James IV Stewart King of Scotland родился 17 марта 1473 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени James III Stewart King of Scotland было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland 8 августа 1503 г. в местности холируд, мидлотян, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. В 1502 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James IV Stewart King of Scotland nació el 17 de marzo de 1473, en Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, su papá, James III Stewart King of Scotland, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margaret Of Denmark Oldenburg, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Margaret Tudor Queen Consort of Scotland el 8 de agosto de 1503, en Holyrood, Midlothian, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1502. Murió el 9 de septiembre de 1513, en Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
